danger_signals.visual_form: claymation character performing strike or projectile motion
clay body distortion, compression, or impact deformation
character flying backward or upward after hit
visual reduction of life bar or health indicator

safety_signals.visual_form: character in neutral standing position, no active animations
safety_signals.meaning: momentary reprieve between exchanges; neutral game state

safety_signals.visual_form: screen space separation between character models
safety_signals.meaning: temporary safety from melee attacks; reset window

safety_signals.visual_form: character animation showing defensive stance or guard

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: each character is a distinct clay-animated archetype (snowman, blob, clown, scarecrow, taffy, rabbit, wrestler)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: circus/carnival arena setting; implied through character roster and thematic cohesion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: snowman form; main character across all cover art
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: primary protagonist identity; recurring across series

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: can transform into bomb (disabled move); morphic clay body
clown makeup and costume; appears in 4 of 5 games

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: fighting arena with two characters facing off; health bars visible; circus-themed stage backdrop
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: characters perform attack animations, defensive stances, and knockback reactions; turn-based exchange of aggression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: health meter depleted to zero; character in prone or incapacitated animation state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: no further attack response; loss animations; potential knockout or elimination visual

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: both characters in neutral idle stance; screen shows arena environment; health bars at normal levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: waiting animation loops; no active aggression; players waiting for input

environmental_storytelling.example: Diverse cast of circus archetypes (Bad Mr. Frosty snowman, green Blob, evil Bonker clown, Ickybod Clay scarecrow, Taffy candy, Hoppy rabbit, Tiny wrestler) establishes world as carnival/circus setting without explicit dialogue

Character designs reference and parody fighting game conventions and broader pop culture (Hoppy as Terminator spoof, Earthworm Jim crossover character), suggesting a lighthearted, self-aware carnival world

All sprites rendered from clay animation creates cohesive visual language—characters visibly deform, compress, and morph during combat, reinforcing that this is a malleable, surreal clay-based world rather than realistic fighter

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: attacks opponent; subject to damage and knockback animations
can transform into bomb that explodes (disabled in final version); indicates shapeshifting threat capability
appears in 4 of 5 games; consistent presence suggests status as recurring antagonist or main rival
cycles between attack, defense, knockback, and idle animations; health meter reduction indicates accumulating damage

notes: ClayFighter is a parody fighting game with minimal narrative worldbuilding. World-state communication relies almost entirely on combat animations, health indicators, and claymation visual differentiation rather than environmental storytelling or factional politics. The 'circus freaks fighting for King of the Circus' premise is mentioned only in plot description, not visually manifested. Character roster itself becomes the primary narrative device—each character's clay-sculpted form communicates their archetype (snowman, blob, clown, scarecrow, taffy, rabbit, wrestler). No environmental hazards, faction banners, or political mechanics documented. Game operates in pure fighting game paradigm without resource management or world-state evolution systems. Blob's disabled bomb transformation suggests original design aimed for more environmental/state-change mechanics.
